accurate.why: The claims present (proposing a constitutional amendment establishing a fundamental right to vote for citizens of legal voting age in their jurisdiction) accurately match the bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims appear in the summary text provided.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly identifies the bill's purpose as proposing a constitutional amendment to establish a fundamental right to vote.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary is truncated before reaching the repeal of the 'or other crime' phrase in Section 2 of the Fourteenth Amendment, omitting a key change to existing law.

exceptions_conditions.why: The summary cuts off before describing the compelling-governmental-interest/least-restrictive-means standard governing denial or abridgment, a material condition.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective dates beyond the standard Article V ratification language, so this criterion does not apply.

major_provisions_covered.why: Because it is cut off mid-sentence, the summary fails to cover the strict-scrutiny standard (Section 2), the Fourteenth Amendment repeal (Section 3), and the congressional enforcement power (Section 4).

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary ends abruptly mid-sentence ('The resolution prohibits the United'), leaving it incomplete and not internally finished.

concise.why: The text contains no padding or filler within the portion provided.

correct_entities.why: The resolution is correctly identified as Senate Joint Resolution 186 proposing a constitutional amendment.

correct_figures.why: The summary states no dollar amounts, dates, or quantities to evaluate.
